response:
[
  {
    "vc_id": 64655,
    "reference": "Psa 79:6",
    "strongs": "H2534",
    "transliteration": "che.mah",
    "gloss": "rage",
    "meaning": "God's rage is directed outward toward nations that neither know him nor call on his name; the psalmist petitions for it to be poured out on them rather than on Israel."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 64798,
    "reference": "Psa 85:3",
    "strongs": "H2740",
    "transliteration": "cha.ron",
    "gloss": "burning anger",
    "meaning": "God's burning anger is portrayed as something that can be turned away and withdrawn; its cessation is the basis for Israel's hope of restored favor."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 925,
    "reference": "Psa 85:4",
    "strongs": "H3708B",
    "transliteration": "ka.a.s",
    "gloss": "vexation",
    "meaning": "God's indignation is directed at his people and needs to be put away for restoration to happen; the psalmist pleads for its removal as a condition of salvation."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 1814,
    "reference": "Psa 85:5",
    "strongs": "H0599",
    "transliteration": "a.naph",
    "gloss": "be angry",
    "meaning": "God's anger is questioned in its duration—will it be forever, across all generations?—revealing it as a sustained inner disposition that has relational consequences for his people."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 64656,
    "reference": "Psa 88:7",
    "strongs": "H2534",
    "transliteration": "che.mah",
    "gloss": "rage",
    "meaning": "God's rage is experienced as a crushing physical and emotional weight pressing down on the psalmist, leaving him overwhelmed as if by surging waves."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 64799,
    "reference": "Psa 88:16",
    "strongs": "H2740",
    "transliteration": "cha.ron",
    "gloss": "burning anger",
    "meaning": "God's burning anger is felt as a sweeping, destroying force that assaults the psalmist from within; its impact is described as dreadful and personally devastating."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 64657,
    "reference": "Psa 89:46",
    "strongs": "H2534",
    "transliteration": "che.mah",
    "gloss": "rage",
    "meaning": "God's rage is depicted as an enduring, intense fire burning against his anointed; the psalmist laments its unrelenting duration and God's apparent hiddenness alongside it."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 64658,
    "reference": "Psa 90:7",
    "strongs": "H2534",
    "transliteration": "che.mah",
    "gloss": "rage",
    "meaning": "God's rage produces a state of dismay and complete undoing in those it strikes; it operates in tandem with his anger to bring human life to its end."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 558,
    "reference": "Psa 102:10",
    "strongs": "H2195",
    "transliteration": "za.am",
    "gloss": "indignation",
    "meaning": "God's indignation is the cause of the psalmist's suffering—being lifted up and thrown down—showing it as a force that produces violent reversal of one's condition."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 64925,
    "reference": "Psa 102:10",
    "strongs": "H7110A",
    "transliteration": "qe.tseph",
    "gloss": "wrath",
    "meaning": "God's wrath, paired with his indignation, is assigned as the reason the psalmist has been violently cast down; it operates as the active agent behind his affliction."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 14250,
    "reference": "Psa 106:16",
    "strongs": "H7065",
    "transliteration": "qa.na",
    "gloss": "be jealous",
    "meaning": "Men in the camp harbored jealousy toward Moses and Aaron, directing envy at those set apart as holy; the jealousy was a communal inner attitude turned against divinely chosen leaders."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 64659,
    "reference": "Psa 106:23",
    "strongs": "H2534",
    "transliteration": "che.mah",
    "gloss": "rage",
    "meaning": "God's rage was directed toward destroying Israel, but Moses' intercession turned it away; the verse shows the rage as potentially lethal but capable of being diverted by advocacy."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 64831,
    "reference": "Psa 106:29",
    "strongs": "H3707",
    "transliteration": "ka.as",
    "gloss": "to provoke",
    "meaning": "Israel's deeds provoked God to anger, triggering a plague; the provocation is the human action that stirs up divine wrath, with immediate destructive consequence."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 64899,
    "reference": "Psa 106:32",
    "strongs": "H7107",
    "transliteration": "qa.tsaph",
    "gloss": "be angry",
    "meaning": "Israel angered God at Meribah through their contention, and this anger spilled over to affect even Moses; it shows anger aroused by human rebellion producing harm beyond its immediate cause."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 64781,
    "reference": "Psa 106:40",
    "strongs": "H2734",
    "transliteration": "cha.rah",
    "gloss": "to be incensed",
    "meaning": "The Lord's anger was kindled against his own people as a response to their persistent unfaithfulness, resulting in his abhorrence of them; incensed feeling led to relational rupture."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 64832,
    "reference": "Psa 112:10",
    "strongs": "H3707",
    "transliteration": "ka.as",
    "gloss": "to provoke",
    "meaning": "The wicked man is provoked to anger upon seeing the righteous prosper; this vexation is an involuntary inner reaction to the good fortune of others, paired with visible gnashing and wasting away."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 21708,
    "reference": "Psa 119:53",
    "strongs": "H2152",
    "transliteration": "zal.a.phah",
    "gloss": "scorching",
    "meaning": "A scorching, burning indignation seizes the psalmist from within when he sees the wicked abandon God's law; it is an intense, consuming inner heat provoked by moral outrage."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 14291,
    "reference": "Psa 119:139",
    "strongs": "H7068",
    "transliteration": "qin.ah",
    "gloss": "jealousy",
    "meaning": "The psalmist's zeal for God's words consumes him inwardly because his enemies disregard them; this jealous passion operates as a devouring inner fire directed at protecting God's honor."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 13146,
    "reference": "Psa 119:154",
    "strongs": "H7379",
    "transliteration": "riv",
    "gloss": "strife",
    "meaning": "The psalmist calls on God to plead his legal cause; here 'riv' denotes a formal dispute or lawsuit in which the psalmist is embattled and needs a divine advocate."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 64782,
    "reference": "Psa 124:3",
    "strongs": "H2734",
    "transliteration": "cha.rah",
    "gloss": "to be incensed",
    "meaning": "Enemies' anger was kindled against Israel to the point of consuming them alive; the verse frames this incensed rage as a near-fatal external threat from which only God's intervention saved them."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 12817,
    "reference": "Psa 139:5",
    "strongs": "H6696B",
    "transliteration": "tsur",
    "gloss": "to provoke",
    "meaning": "This verse describes God hemming the psalmist in on all sides with his hand upon him; it does not evidently express provocation or inner contention in this context."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 14251,
    "reference": "Pro 3:31",
    "strongs": "H7065",
    "transliteration": "qa.na",
    "gloss": "be jealous",
    "meaning": "The command warns against envying a violent man; jealousy here is an inward disposition of coveting another's power or lifestyle that leads one to imitate destructive ways."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 64661,
    "reference": "Pro 6:34",
    "strongs": "H2534",
    "transliteration": "che.mah",
    "gloss": "rage",
    "meaning": "Jealousy ignites a man's rage to the point where he shows no mercy in taking revenge; the rage is the explosive, unsparing inner fire produced by jealousy's wound."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 14283,
    "reference": "Pro 6:34",
    "strongs": "H7068",
    "transliteration": "qin.ah",
    "gloss": "jealousy",
    "meaning": "Jealousy is named as the force that makes a man furious and drives him to merciless revenge; it is an inner passion that generates uncontrollable anger and retaliatory action."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 64846,
    "reference": "Pro 12:16",
    "strongs": "H3708B",
    "transliteration": "ka.a.s",
    "gloss": "vexation",
    "meaning": "A fool's vexation is instantly visible, betraying his inner irritation without restraint; the verse contrasts this with the prudent person who conceals offence, showing vexation as a self-exposing inner reaction."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 38083,
    "reference": "Pro 13:10",
    "strongs": "H4683",
    "transliteration": "mats.tsah",
    "gloss": "strife",
    "meaning": "Strife is the invariable product of insolence, while wisdom belongs to those who accept counsel; the verse roots strife in a proud inner refusal to submit to others' input."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 14277,
    "reference": "Pro 14:30",
    "strongs": "H7068",
    "transliteration": "qin.ah",
    "gloss": "jealousy",
    "meaning": "Envy is contrasted with a tranquil heart—where tranquility gives life, envy rots the bones; jealousy is a deeply corrosive inner state that physically and vitally destroys the person who harbors it."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 591,
    "reference": "Pro 15:1",
    "strongs": "H2534",
    "transliteration": "che.mah",
    "gloss": "rage",
    "meaning": "Rage can be turned away by a soft answer but is stirred up by harsh words; the verse shows rage as a volatile inner state highly responsive to the relational tone of speech directed at it."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 64662,
    "reference": "Pro 15:18",
    "strongs": "H2534",
    "transliteration": "che.mah",
    "gloss": "rage",
    "meaning": "A hot-tempered man's inner rage stirs up strife around him, while the slow-to-anger person quiets conflict; the verse identifies rage as the relational agitator whose presence escalates contention."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 13126,
    "reference": "Pro 15:18",
    "strongs": "H7379",
    "transliteration": "riv",
    "gloss": "strife",
    "meaning": "Strife is what a hot-tempered man generates and what a patient man quiets; it is the relational conflict that flares when anger is uncontrolled and subsides when anger is restrained."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 64663,
    "reference": "Pro 16:14",
    "strongs": "H2534",
    "transliteration": "che.mah",
    "gloss": "rage",
    "meaning": "A king's rage is a lethal force—a messenger of death—but a wise man works to appease it; the verse presents royal rage as an inner disposition with life-and-death power over others."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 13127,
    "reference": "Pro 17:1",
    "strongs": "H7379",
    "transliteration": "riv",
    "gloss": "strife",
    "meaning": "Strife is set against quiet as the defining evil of a household; even abundance with strife is worse than a dry crust with peace, showing strife as a toxic relational atmosphere that poisons everything."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 13128,
    "reference": "Pro 17:14",
    "strongs": "H7379",
    "transliteration": "riv",
    "gloss": "strife",
    "meaning": "Strife is like water breaking through a dam—once started it cannot be contained; the verse urges abandoning it at the first sign, showing it as an inner-relational force with escalating, destructive momentum."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 38084,
    "reference": "Pro 17:19",
    "strongs": "H4683",
    "transliteration": "mats.tsah",
    "gloss": "strife",
    "meaning": "Loving strife is equated with loving transgression; strife is here an inner appetite—a willful disposition toward conflict—that is morally aligned with sin and leads to self-destruction."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 926,
    "reference": "Pro 17:25",
    "strongs": "H3708B",
    "transliteration": "ka.a.s",
    "gloss": "vexation",
    "meaning": "A foolish son is a grief and a source of bitterness; the vexation is the painful inner distress caused in the parent by a child's folly, felt as both grief and bitter anguish."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 13129,
    "reference": "Pro 18:6",
    "strongs": "H7379",
    "transliteration": "riv",
    "gloss": "strife",
    "meaning": "A fool's lips lead him into a fight and invite physical punishment; strife here is the conflict that a fool's undisciplined speech inevitably produces, with the inner folly expressed through contentious words."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 64948,
    "reference": "Pro 18:17",
    "strongs": "H7379",
    "transliteration": "riv",
    "gloss": "strife",
    "meaning": "The one who presents their case first appears right until the other party cross-examines them; 'riv' here refers to a legal dispute or case, where the inner dynamic is one of contested claims needing scrutiny."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 46123,
    "reference": "Pro 19:3",
    "strongs": "H2196",
    "transliteration": "za.aph",
    "gloss": "to enrage",
    "meaning": "When a man's own folly ruins him, his heart rages against the Lord rather than himself; the heart is the seat of this misdirected enraging inner reaction, displacing blame onto God."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 46128,
    "reference": "Pro 19:12",
    "strongs": "H2197",
    "transliteration": "za.aph",
    "gloss": "rage",
    "meaning": "A king's rage is compared to a lion's growl—threatening and fearsome—contrasted with the life-giving gentleness of his favor; rage is a powerful inner state with intimidating outward force."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 587,
    "reference": "Pro 19:19",
    "strongs": "H2534",
    "transliteration": "che.mah",
    "gloss": "rage",
    "meaning": "A man of great rage bears consequences he cannot escape, and rescuing him only perpetuates the cycle; the verse shows rage as a deeply ingrained inner disposition that repeatedly brings its bearer into trouble."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 13130,
    "reference": "Pro 20:3",
    "strongs": "H7379",
    "transliteration": "riv",
    "gloss": "strife",
    "meaning": "Keeping away from strife is honorable, while only fools rush into quarreling; the verse frames strife as an inner-relational pull that the wise resist and the foolish cannot avoid."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 592,
    "reference": "Pro 21:14",
    "strongs": "H2534",
    "transliteration": "che.mah",
    "gloss": "rage",
    "meaning": "A hidden bribe can appease strong rage; the verse treats rage as an inner force that can be secretly pacified, showing it as a formidable but manageable disposition."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 927,
    "reference": "Pro 21:19",
    "strongs": "H3708B",
    "transliteration": "ka.a.s",
    "gloss": "vexation",
    "meaning": "Living with a quarrelsome and fretful woman is so vexatious that isolation in a desert is preferable; the vexation is a constant, wearing inner irritation caused by contentious companionship."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 545,
    "reference": "Pro 22:14",
    "strongs": "H2194",
    "transliteration": "za.am",
    "gloss": "be indignant",
    "meaning": "The Lord's indignation toward a man is the inner disposition that leaves him vulnerable to the trap of the forbidden woman; divine displeasure is the condition that leads to the man's downfall."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 13157,
    "reference": "Pro 22:23",
    "strongs": "H7379",
    "transliteration": "riv",
    "gloss": "strife",
    "meaning": "God will plead the cause of the poor against those who exploit them; 'riv' here is a legal contest in which the Lord takes up the disadvantaged party's case, turning their strife into divine advocacy."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 64664,
    "reference": "Pro 22:24",
    "strongs": "H2534",
    "transliteration": "che.mah",
    "gloss": "rage",
    "meaning": "One is warned against befriending a man given to rage, because association with a wrathful person risks absorbing their destructive inner patterns; rage is infectious through close companionship."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 13158,
    "reference": "Pro 23:11",
    "strongs": "H7379",
    "transliteration": "riv",
    "gloss": "strife",
    "meaning": "God as the strong Redeemer will plead the cause of the vulnerable against those who wrong them; 'riv' denotes the legal-adversarial dispute in which God himself becomes the champion."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 14252,
    "reference": "Pro 23:17",
    "strongs": "H7065",
    "transliteration": "qa.na",
    "gloss": "be jealous",
    "meaning": "The heart is warned not to envy sinners; jealousy of the wicked is an inner disposition of the heart that diverts one from fearing God and must be actively resisted."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 14253,
    "reference": "Pro 24:1",
    "strongs": "H7065",
    "transliteration": "qa.na",
    "gloss": "be jealous",
    "meaning": "The reader is told not to envy evil men or desire their company; jealousy here is an inner attraction toward the wicked that the will must resist, lest one be drawn into evil."
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 64783,
    "reference": "Pro 24:19",
    "strongs": "H2734",
    "transliteration": "cha.rah",
    "gloss": "to be incensed",
    "meaning": "The reader is told not to fret or become internally agitated over evildoers; the inner disposition of being incensed at the wicked is something to be deliberately restrained rather than indulged."
  }
]
